RULES 3.1179 AND 3.1184 IS DENIED. THE PRIOR ORDER SOUGHT TO BE VACATED IS NOT VOID FOR LACK OF JURISDICTION IN ANY RESPECT. EVEN IF ANY OF DEFENDANTS' ARGUMENTS WERE SUBSTANTIVELY CORRECT, WHICH THEY DO NOT APPEAR TO BE, NONE OF THOSE ARGUMENTS GO TO THE JURISDICTION OF THE COURT TO ISSUE THE PRIOR ORDER. THE COURT DID NOT LACK JURISDICTION TO APPOINT A RECEIVER OR TO PROVIDE THE RECEIVER WITH THE AUTHORITY TO TAKE OR NOT TAKE THE ACTIONS THAT HE WAS EMPOWERED TO TAKE. NOR WAS THE COURT'S EXPRESS OR IMPLIED APPROVAL OF THE RECEIVER'S REPORT AND ACCOUNT AND ACTIONS TAKEN BY OR AUTHORIZED TO BE TAKEN BY THE RECEIVER MADE WITHOUT JURISDICTION. NOR DOES THE PRIOR ORDER VIOLATE DUE PROCESS. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE HAROLD E. KAHN; CLERK: VANESSA WU; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

MOTION / Notce Of Motion & Motion To Vacate Order Of November 26, 2024, For Failure To Adjudicate Jurisdictional Challenges; Violation Of Cal. Rules 3.1179 And 3.1184
Off Calendar Jun-04-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances

Michael Weiss, Deputy City Attorney, 415-554-3824, via zoom for plaintiff
Anne Kihagi, defendant in pro per, via zoom
Karen Uchiyama, Law office of Karen Uchiyama, 415-271-3364, via zoom for all other defendants
Danielle Gabai, Ervin Cohen & Jessup LLP, 310-273-6333, via zoom for receiver Kevin Singer

Ms. Kihagi contests to the tentative ruling. Before discussion, Judge Kahn disclosed that in 2016, he presided over a settlement conference in this case and met separately with the parties. That was before the trial and long before the appointment of the receiver. Judge Kahn stated that he did not believe that he was disqualified from hearing this motion. Judge Kahn further states that he believes he was and could be fair, neutral and impartial to all parties in this case. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ANNE KIHAGI AKA ANNA KIHAGI AKA ANNA SWAIN AKA ANNE KIHAGI SWAIN AKA ANNA KIHAGI SWAIN, XELAN PROP 1, LLC, RENKA PROP, LLC, NOZARI 2, LLC, ZORIALL, LLC MOTION / NOTCE OF MOTION & MOTION TO VACATE ORDER OF NOVEMBER 26, 2024, FOR FAILURE TO ADJUDICATE JURISDICTIONAL CHALLENGES; VIOLATION OF CAL. RULES 3.1179 AND 3.1184

is DENIED. The prior order sought to be vacated is not void for lack of jurisdiction in any respect. Even if any of defendants' arguments were substantively correct, which they do not appear to be, none of those arguments go to the jurisdiction of the court to issue the prior order. The court did not lack jurisdiction to appoint a Receiver or to provide the Receiver with the authority to take or not take the actions that he was empowered to take. Nor was the court's express or implied approval of the Receiver's report and account and actions taken by or authorized to be taken by the Receiver made without jurisdiction. Nor does the prior order violate due process.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Not Reported. (D501)

Motion For Order Extending Expiration Date Of Permanent Injunction From May 23, 2022 To July 29, 2024
Off Calendar Apr-06-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Michael Weiss and Peter Keith; Deputy City Attorneys; 415-554-3908; appearing via Zoom on behalf of plaintiffs City and County of San Francisco and People of the State of California.
Blake Alsbrook; Ervin Cohen & Jessup LLP; 310-273-6333; appearing via Zoom on behalf of receiver Kevin Singer.
Anne Kihagi; 415-691-1264; defendant in pro per, appearing via CourtCall on her own behalf.
Karen Uchiyama; 415-563-9300; appearing via CourtCall on behalf of defendants Julia Mwangi, Christine Mwangi, Xelan Prop 1 LLC, Renka Prop LLC, Nozari 2 LLC and Zoriall LLC.

Plaintiffs contest the court's tentative ruling. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court rules as follows:

PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER EXTENDING EXPIRATION DATE OF PERMANENT INJUNCTION FROM MAY 23, 2022 TO JULY 29, 2024 is DENIED without prejudice. Plaintiffs do not allege any new allegations of new violations. The Court is not inclined to extend the Injunction based on violations that occurred almost three years ago when Plaintiffs could have brought a request to extend the Injunction in 2019 when they moved to enforce the Injunction. Second, Plaintiffs do not cite any authority for their proposition that an appeal staying the terms of an Injunction is grounds for a Court to extend an Injunction. Plaintiff and Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

Karen Y. Uchiyama; 415-563-9300; appearing on behalf of defendants Julia Mwangi, Christine Mwangi, Xelan Prop 1, LLc, Renka Prop, LLC, Nozari 2, LLC and Zoriall, LLC.
Blake Alsbrook; Ervin Cohen & Jessup LLP; 310-273-6333; appearing via Zoom on behalf of receiver Kevin Singer.
Michael S. Weiss; Deputy City Attorney; 415-554-3908; appearing via Zoom on behalf of plaintiffs City and County of San Francisco and People of the State of California.

Not contested. The court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS' NOTICE OF MOTION FOR COURT ORDER TO DETERMINE NECESSITY OF APPEAL BOND TO STAY RECEIVER SALE OF PROPERTY PENDING APPEAL OF POST-JUDGMENT COLLATERAL SALE ORDER is GRANTED in part and MOOT in part. The moving party requests a determination of necessity of appeal bond. The posting of a bond or undertaking is required in order to stay receivership proceedings pending an appeal. (Code Civ. Proc., 917.4 [undertaking required to stay sale of property in receivership]; City of Riverside v. Horspool (2014) 223 Cal.App.4th 670, 672). See CCSF v. Kihagi A162609 November 2, 2021 order. To the extent the motion seeks the determination of the bond amount, the motion is moot in light of January 4, 2022 sale. After the property has been sold there is no relief a trial court can grant. (City of Riverside at 682).

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

DEFENDANT ANNE KIHAGI'S NOTICE OF MOTION TO SET-ASIDE RULINGS OF JULY 23, 2021, MARCH 1, 2021 AND APRIL 7, 2021 DUE TO JUDICIAL BAD FAITH UNDER SUPREME COURT AUTHORITY IN "BROADMAN" DECISION is DENIED. Authorities cited in support of this motion do not provide for procedural mechanism to vacate these orders at the trial level on the grounds stated in the motion. While it is possible that a trial judge may make erroneous rulings while carefully considering parties' respective briefs and arguments on issues unsupported by any precedent on point both procedurally and substantively, there are limited procedural mechanisms to address such errors at the trial level, e.g. motion for reconsideration, motion for a new trial, etc.

The issues of jurisdiction and discovery sanctions have been determined on appeal. Moving party failed to cite any case where a trial Court vacated its discovery order that had been affirmed on appeal or where a trial Court vacated its judgment as void for lack of jurisdiction after the judgment was affirmed on appeal to support its requested relief for the Court to "make an appropriate order vacating the sanction order as void, and appropriately reversing all appropriate parts of the judgment that arose from that order." Notice Of Defendant Motion - For An Order Denying Receivers Request For Payments & An Order To Turn-Over All Accounts And Operations Of Receiver Pending Decisions On Appeal
Off Calendar Dec-15-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Peter Keith and Michael Weiss; Deputy City Attorneys; 415-554-3908; appearing via Zoom on behalf of plaintiffs City and County of San Francisco and People of the State of California.
Anne Kihagi; 415-691-1264; defendant in pro per, appearing via Zoom on their own behalf.
Karen Uchiyama; 415-563-9300; appearing via CourtCall on behalf of defendants Julia Mwangi, Christine Mwangi, Xelan Prop 1, LLC, et al.
Blake C. Alsbrook; Ervin Cohen & Jessup LLP; 310-273-6333; appearing via Zoom on behalf of receiver Kevin Singer.
Ravi D. Sahae; Leland Parachini Steinberg Matzger & Melnick, LLP; 415-957-1800; appearing via Zoom on behalf of interested parties Lin Dee Liu dba Lin Dee Liu Services, et al.

In attendance: Kevin Singer, receiver; Ed Deleski, realtor; Rick Marquis, project manager.

Court reporter: Dianne McGivern, CSR #7576, dianne@trialcsr.com, 310-930-4096.

Defendants contest the court's tentative ruling. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ANNE KIHAGI'S NOTICE OF DEFENDANT MOTION FOR AN ORDER DENYING RECEIVER'S REQUEST FOR PAYMENTS & AN ORDER TO TURN-OVER ALL ACCOUNTS AND OPERATIONS OF RECEIVER PENDING DECISIONS ON APPEAL is DENIED. As this Court has previously explained on multiple occasions the appeal from orders denying Defendants motions to terminate/void/vacate, etc. the order appointing the receiver years after the receiver took over the estate does not create a stay because as far as those orders are concerned, there is nothing to stay. By those decisions the defendants had been denied the affirmative relief which they sought. The decisions required no process of the Court to carry them into effect. As the Court has previously stated on the record the ruling could have been different had defendant appealed the original order appointing the receiver before the received took over. At this time, and based on the procedural posture of the case, the request to order the receiver to turn over all accounts and operations is denied. The motion for order denying receiver request for payments is denied as procedurally improper. In the future Defendant shall follow procedures outlined in CRC 3.1183 to object to receiver's report and accounting.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Court reporter: Dianne McGivern, CSR #7576, dianne@trialcsr.com, 310-930-4096. (D501)

RULING - A R G U E D. Defense Counsel argues. Receiver's Counsel responds. Ms. Kihagi argues and cites case authority. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT ANNE KIHAGI, JULIA MWANGI, XELAN PROP 1, LLC, RENKA PROP, LLC, NOZARI 2, LLC, ZORIALL, LLC, CHRISTINE MWANGI's Motion To Vacate Court Order Of March 1st 2021 Expunging Lis Pendens.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Motion is DENIED. Motion under CCP 1008(b) is not proper. To the extent the motion is based on CCP 1008(a), it is untimely. Moving party fails to cite any authority for the proposition that relief under CCP 473(d) is available under the circumstances; the two cases cited in the moving papers are not on point. To the extent Defendant insists that on March 1, 2021 the Court should have made a ruling on the Motion to Expunge under CCP 405.31 and 405.32, the motion would be denied as Defendant failed to carry its burden to show by preponderance of evidence probable validity of real property claims, if any. See February 16, 2021 filings in support of opposition to the Motion to Expunge Lis Pendens containing no evidence.

Mr. Alsbrook to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Judge: Vedica Puri, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court reporter: Florence Colby, CSR #12433, 559-381-2376, rcflojo@gmail.com, Reported. (210)

MINI MINUTES: Appearances by ZOOM
Blake Alsbrook, Ervin Cohen & Jessup LLP, 310-273-6333 for Receiver Kevin Singer.
Karen Uchiyama, 415-563-9300, for Defendants Julia Mwangi, et al
Anne Kihagi, 415-691-1264, Self-represented defendant on her own behalf (Present on Zoom as a listener)
Kevin Singer, (Present on Zoom as a listener)

Court addresses with counsel the Court's tentative ruling. Court states that she has reviewed the additional briefings submitted this morning. Ms. Uchiyama states that she was unaware of the Court's tentative ruling. Mr. Alsbrook states that the additional briefings requested by the Court were served to Ms. Uchiyama at 10 p.m. on Sunday evening by email. Ms. Uchiyama states that she did not read her email today and has not received the additional briefings, but that she is ready to proceed.

Court states to counsel that the Receivers' motion to Expunge lis Pendens is based on 2 arguments.

1. The Kihagi Defendants have no right to encumber the receivership property;
2. The Kihagi Defendants case against Chase cannot support a Lis Pendens.

The Kihagi defendants were required to request permission before filing a lis pendens encumbering the property but did not do so.

Hearing is held. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Receiver's Motion To Expunge Lis Pendens is GRANTED. Mr. Alsbrook to submit an order.

Judge: Vedica Puri; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported. (D210)

MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Michael Weiss, San Francisco City Attorney's Office, (415)554-3824 - appearing on behalf of plaintiff City & County of San Francisco, et al.
David Cook, Cook Collection, (415)989-4730 - appearing on behalf of plaintiff City & County of San Francisco, et al.
Blake Alsbrook, Ervin Cohen & Jessup LLP, 310-273-6333 - appearing on behalf of Reciever Kevin Singer.
Karen Uchiyama, (415)563-9300 - appearing on behalf of defendants Julia Mwangi, et al.
Anne Kihagi, (415)691-1264 - self-represented defendant appearing on her own behalf.
REPORTER: Florence Colby, CSR# 12433, ucflojo@gmail.com

Argued. The court discusses Ms. Uchiyama's brief filed on 2/1/2021, titled "Co-defendants' Brief Supporting Defendant Khiagi's Motion to Change Post-Judgment Proceedings to Superior Court Outside of San Francisco County." The court admonishes Ms. Uchiyama for the language contained in the brief, specifically on lines 6 through 19 of page 3. The court rules as follows: DEFENDANT ANNE KIHAGI'S MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE is DENIED. Venue is proper in San Francisco under CCP 394, 395 and 397. Moving party failed to cite binding applicable authority for the proposition that enforcement of judgment is a separate proceeding from the underlying action giving rise to the claim that venue is no longer proper as Defendants' claimed residency changed since 2015. Venue is determined at the commencement of the action. Defendants failed to carry their burden under CCP 397. Plaintiff's request for fees is DENIED. Co-Defendants' Brief Supporting Defendant Kihagi's Motion to Change Post-Judgment Proceedings to Superior Court Outside of San Francisco County, filed on 2/1/2021 is stricken. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Vedica Puri; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Reporter: Florence Colby, CSR# 12433, ucflojo@gmail.com (D501)

The Court makes other orders as set forth in the transcript of the court reporter. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Issue, Evidentiary, Terminating, And Monetary Sanctions Against Defendants And To Enforce Courts June 10, 2016 Order
Off Calendar Jul-21-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Deputy City Attorney, Victoria Weatherford (415-554-3970) appears for plaintiff/opposing party, City and County of San Francisco

Attorney, John H. Cigavic III, Esq. of Basic Legal Services (415-859-5065) makes special appearance for defendants/moving parties, Anne Kihagi, Julia Mwangi and Christine Mwangi

Julie Nong of The NT Law Group (949-351-6327) appears telephonically for defendants/moving parties, Anne Kihagi, Julia Mwangi and Christine Mwangi

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides. The Court confirms the tentative ruling with modifications as set forth in the transcript of the court reporter. PLAINTIFFS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO and THE PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA'S Motion For Issue, Evidentiary, Terminating, And Monetary Sanctions Against Defendants And To Enforce Courts June 10, 2016 Order is granted in part as to evidentiary sanctions and denied in its entirety as to issue, terminating and monetary sanctions. Defendants are prohibited from introducing any evidence in any motion papers, at any hearing or at trial that was requested in plaintiffs' first set of form interrogatories except: 1) any information that defendants first learned about after their service of the most recent version of their responses to those interrogatories and 2) any information covered by their pending motion for a protective order. The asserted non-payment of the prior sanctions award and the asserted failure to serve "wet" verifications, even if they have not been remedied, are not failures warranting the relief sought by this motion other than to re-affirm defendants should comply with those portions of the June 10 order immediately. The heart of this motion is the asserted non-compliance with the portion of June10, 2016 order requiring defendants to serve "Code-compliant amended responses, without objections, to Plaintiffs' Form Interrogatories, Set One." Defendants appear to take the position that, but for information for which they have moved for a protective order, they have fully complied with the quoted language of the June 10 order. Plaintiffs appear to take the position that this is incorrect. In the context of this motion, the appropriate course is to take defendants at their word and preclude them from introducing any evidence responsive to the interrogatories other than what is covered by defendants' motion for a protective order. As to the information covered by defendants' motion for a protective order, the protective order motion should be resolved prior to any sanctions imposed relating to that information. Defedants may add to their 15.1 responses as set forth on the record. Compliance by July 29, 2016. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Peggy Tsujimoto, CSR #5229, Bus. Tel: 415-828-1919. (D302)

Defendants Xelan Prop 1, Llc And Zoriall, Llcs And Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief From Waiver Of Discovery Objections
Off Calendar Jul-21-2016 Continued To Jul-28-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Deputy City Attorney, Victoria Weatherford (415-554-3970) appears for plaintiff/opposing party, City and County of San Francisco

Attorney, John H. Cigavic III, Esq. of Basic Legal Services (415-859-5065) makes special appearance for defendants/moving parties, Anne Kihagi, Julia Mwangi and Christine Mwangi

Julie Nong of The NT Law Group (949-351-6327) appears telephonically for defendants/moving parties, Anne Kihagi, Julia Mwangi and Christine Mwangi

Court hears from both sides, finds no opposition, no contest. The Court confirms the tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS JULIA MWANGI, XELAN PROP 1, LLC, RENKA PROP, LLC, NOZARI 2, LLC, ZORIALL, LLC, ANNA KIHAGI and CHRISTINE MWANGI'S Motion For Relief From Waiver Of Discovery Objections is OFF CALENDAR since it appears that the parties have resolved all issues raised by this motion as to Xelan. Defendant Zoriall's motion for relief from waiver of discovery objections is granted on the condition that, to the extent that it has not done so already, Zoriall serve full code-compliant substantive responses to all discovery requests at issue in this motion no later than July 26, 2016. Zoriall has adequately shown that its failure to timely serve discovery responses was the result of mistake, inadvertence or excusable neglect. However, it is not clear whether Zoriall has served code-compliant substantive responses to all of the discovery requests. Zoriall's motion will be continued to July 28, 2016 for a determination of whether Zoriall has complied with the condition for the granting of the motion. If plaintiffs believe that Zoriall has not so complied, plaintiffs' counsel must send an email to Zoriall's counsel and to contestdept302tr@sftc.org no later than 4pm on July 27, 2016 identifying, without argument, each response that plaintiffs believe that Zoriall has not provided a code-compliant substantive response and the hearing will then proceed on July 28. If no such email is received, the court will assume that Zoriall has complied with the condition of the granting of the motion and the motion will then be granted.

Ms. Nong is ordered to provide signed verifications for all discovery being requested. Ms. Cesare-Eastman is to prepare a form of order.

Defendants Julia Mwangi, Xelan Prop 1, Llc, Renka Prop, Llc, Nozari 2, Llc, Anna Kihagi, And Christine Mwangi'S Motion To Strike 1st Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Dec-15-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Megan Cesare-Eastman of Office of The City Attorney telephone number 415-554-3970 for Plaintiff.
Victoria Weatherford of Office of The City Attorney telephone number 415-554-4287 for Plaintiff.
Aleksandr Volkov of Volkov Law Firm telephone number 415-987-7000 for Defendant.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling. Defendants Julia Mwangi, Xelan Prop 1, LLC, Renka Prop, LLC, Nozari 2, LLC, Anna Kihagi, Zoriall, LLC and Christine Mwangi's Motion To Strike 1st Amended Complaint is denied in its entirety. Even if the court were to take judicial notice of the contents of Department of Building Inspection records, defendants are not entitled to have any portions of the first amended complaint stricken. All of the portions of the first amended complaint that defendants seek to strike are evidentiary facts which have independent relevance apart from the DBI records. The doctrines of res judicata and collateral estoppel apply only to claims or issues that have been fully adjudicated. Neither of these doctrines apply to the allegations of evidentiary or facts. In addition, the Court granted the request to take judicial notice as to the Department of Building Inspection records only and denied the request as to the brochure and the complaint. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Irene L. Tofft, CSR #12913. (302/HK)

Defendant Christine Mwangi'S Demurrer To First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Dec-15-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Megan Cesare-Eastman of Office of The City Attorney telephone number 415-554-3970 for Plaintiff.
Victoria Weatherford of Office of The City Attorney telephone number 415-554-4287 for Plaintiff.
Aleksandr Volkov of Volkov Law Firm telephone number 415-987-7000 for Defendant.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling. Defendant Christine Mwangi's Demurrer To First Amended Complaint is overruled in its entirety. While the allegations against Ms. Mwangi are thin, they are sufficient to withstand a demurrer. As Ms. Mwangi suggests in her papers, if she wishes further specificity and if she believes she is entitled to have the claims alleged against her adjudicated in her favor, her recourse is to serve discovery and filed a summary motion. In addition, Defendants have 30-days to answer. Furthermore, the Court granted the request to take judicial notice as to the Department of Building Inspection records only and denied the request as to the brochure and the complaint. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Irene L. Tofft, CSR #12913. (302/HK)

Defendants Nozari 2, Llc, Xelan Prop 1, Llc, Renka Prop, Llc, Zoriall, Llc And Anna Kihagi'S Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Dec-15-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Megan Cesare-Eastman of Office of The City Attorney telephone number 415-554-3970 for Plaintiff.
Victoria Weatherford of Office of The City Attorney telephone number 415-554-4287 for Plaintiff.
Aleksandr Volkov of Volkov Law Firm telephone number 415-987-7000 for Defendant.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling. Defendants Nozari 2, LLC, Xelan Prop 1, LLC, Renka Prop, LLC, Zoriall, LLC, Julia Mwangi and Anna Kihagi's Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint is overruled in its entirety. All of plaintiffs' claims, including their requests for injunctive relief, are adequately alleged. Even if the court were to take judicial notice of the contents of Department of Building Inspection records, all of plaintiffs' claims would still be sufficiently pled. All of the portions of the first amended complaint that defendants challenge by way of the DBI records that they seek to have judicially noticed are evidentiary facts which have independent relevance apart from the DBI records. Many of defendants' arguments are based on their view of the facts, yet on a demurrer the court assumes the truth of the allegations in the pleading that is attacked. In addition, Defendants have 30-days to answer. Furthermore, the Court granted the request to take judicial notice as to the Department of Building Inspection records only and denied the request as to the brochure and the complaint. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Irene L. Tofft, CSR #12913. (302/HK)
